questions.question: What is Bharat known for worldwide?
questions.explanation: Bharat has a reputation for producing wise and heroic individuals.

questions.question: What attracts numerous travellers to Bharat?
questions.explanation: Bharat's unique culture is a major draw for visitors from around the world.

questions.question: What unites the people of Bharat?
questions.explanation: Despite their differences, the people of Bharat live in harmony with one another.

questions.question: Which geographical features are mentioned in the passage?
questions.correct_answer: rivers, lakes, high mountains, green forests
questions.explanation: Bharat is a land with diverse and varied geography.

questions.question: What advice is given to everyone regarding Bharat's prosperity?
questions.explanation: The passage emphasizes the importance of hard work in maintaining Bharat's prosperity.

questions.question: What is Aipan?
questions.correct_answer: folk art that involves drawing with geru (red clay)
questions.explanation: Aipan is a traditional folk art form from Uttarakhand.

questions.question: What is Dhokra?
questions.explanation: Dhokra is an ancient craft practiced by the Odisha tribe.

questions.question: How is Aipan made?
questions.correct_answer: with white rice flour paste on brick-red walls
questions.explanation: Aipan designs are created using a mixture of white rice flour and geru (red clay).

questions.question: What is the Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme all about?
questions.explanation: The programme aims to celebrate the unity that exists among people from different parts of India.

questions.question: Why is the Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme being conducted?
questions.correct_answer: to promote national integration and unity
questions.explanation: The programme seeks to bring together people from diverse backgrounds and foster a sense of national pride.

notes.title: Bharat's reputation worldwide
notes.content: Bharat is known for producing wise and heroic individuals.

notes.title: Importance of hard work in maintaining prosperity
notes.content: Hard work is essential to ensuring Bharat's continued prosperity.

notes.title: Unity in diversity as a strength
notes.content: Bharat's diverse population living together in harmony is its greatest asset.

notes.title: Aipan and Dhokra as traditional folk art forms
notes.content: Aipan and Dhokra are ancient crafts practiced by different communities in India.

notes.title: Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme aims to promote national integration
notes.content: The programme seeks to celebrate the unity that exists among people from different parts of India.

notes.title: Bharat's rich and diverse culture
notes.content: Bharat has a unique cultural heritage that is worth preserving and celebrating.

notes.title: Importance of geographical features in maintaining biodiversity
notes.content: The varied geography of Bharat supports a wide range of flora and fauna.

notes.title: Role of women in traditional crafts
notes.content: Women play an important role in preserving and passing down traditional craft practices like Aipan and Dhokra.

notes.title: Metalcraft as an ancient Indian tradition
notes.content: Dhokra is a 4000-year-old metalcraft practiced by the Odisha tribe.

notes.title: Folk art forms as a means of preserving cultural heritage
notes.content: Aipan and Dhokra are examples of how folk art forms can help preserve India's rich cultural diversity.

facts.fact: Bharat is known for producing wise and heroic individuals worldwide.
The Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme promotes national integration and unity among Indians from diverse backgrounds.
Aipan is a traditional folk art form from Uttarakhand that involves drawing with geru (red clay).
Dhokra is a 4000-year-old metalcraft practiced by the Odisha tribe, which involves melting wax and pouring liquid metal into it.
Bharat's diverse geography includes rivers, lakes, high mountains, and green forests.
The Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme is an initiative to celebrate the unity that exists among people from different parts of India.
Aipan designs are created using a mixture of white rice flour and geru (red clay).
Dhokra is an ancient craft practiced by the Odisha tribe, which has been passed down through generations.
The Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme aims to promote national integration and unity among Indians from diverse backgrounds.
Bharat's rich cultural heritage is reflected in its traditional folk art forms like Aipan and Dhokra.
